metric_value: $4 billion in sales during last four quarters
source_page: Cisco: At The Core of the Network
notes: Confirms Cisco's scale at time of report; Cisco FY1997 revenue was approximately $6.4B.

metric_value: $40 billion market capitalization
source_page: Cisco: At The Core of the Network
notes: At time of report; Cisco market cap would peak at ~$555B in March 2000.

metric_value: Over 40% of the LAN switching market
notes: Aberdeen confirms Cisco had already captured >40% of LAN switching through acquisitions and rollouts.

metric_name: Cisco's share of Internet backbone traffic
metric_value: More than 80% of Internet backbone traffic carried by Cisco routers
source_page: Focus on The Internet Service Provider
notes: Confirms Cisco's extraordinary dominance in internet routing infrastructure.

metric_name: IOS as de facto networking standard
metric_value: Cisco IOS described as de facto standard in internetworking; unifying thread across disparate networks; embedded in hardware, server software, client OS
notes: Aberdeen validates IOS's market-standard position.

metric_value: Implemented in 7000/7500 series routers; per-port enabling; flow cache expires inactive flows; 10% overhead on router processing
notes: Aberdeen documents NetFlow's capabilities and limitations including the 10% overhead tradeoff.

metric_value: Establishes flow between source/destination on first packet; subsequent packets switched not routed; exports per-conversation statistics; enables per-flow QoS and security
notes: Aberdeen correctly identifies NetFlow's two core values: performance improvement and management data.

metric_name: Tag Switching status at time of report
metric_value: Not yet available for field trials in early 1997; submitted to IETF; endorsed by numerous third parties
notes: Aberdeen notes the technology was not yet field-ready — important accuracy caveat.

metric_value: Combines Layer 2 switching performance with Layer 3 routing scalability; eliminates connection set-up congestion; pre-establishes accelerated paths; enables ATM switch participation in Layer 3 routing
notes: Aberdeen accurately describes MPLS's fundamental architectural value proposition.

metric_value: Multigigabit crossbar switching fabric sustaining 80 Gbps; high port density; interfaces OC-3, OC-12, OC-48; Gigabit Ethernet, Packet over SONET, ATM; fully distributed forwarding; topology-driven route-table switching
notes: Aberdeen documents the GSR's ambitious specifications for next-generation internet routing.

metric_value: High-performance ASICs for ATM switching; complete ABR support; virtual path trunking; weighted fair queuing; multicast; higher density lower cost ATM switching
notes: StrataCom's ASIC technology was the first synergy output of Cisco/StrataCom combination.

metric_value: Four-fold increase in system capacity from new high-performance system processors and high-speed interfaces
notes: Aberdeen documents the 4x capacity improvement from combined Cisco/StrataCom engineering.

metric_value: End-to-end internetworking solutions provider; assimilating switching into historically router-centric portfolio; acquiring promising technologies ($40B market cap as acquisition currency)
notes: Aberdeen documents Cisco's dual strategy: router leadership + aggressive switching integration.

metric_value: Aberdeen predicts Cisco will maintain dominant position by assimilating switching into its portfolio; IOS investment protection retains installed base; management committed to full-service provider status
notes: Aberdeen's central prediction: Cisco will not be displaced by switching technology.

metric_value: Tag Switching promises significant features for internet and enterprise scaling; submitted to IETF; uncertainty about whether it will achieve IOS-level industry-standard enthusiasm
notes: Aberdeen correctly hedges: Tag Switching promise is real but standardization outcome uncertain.

metric_name: GSR as appropriate and necessary product
metric_value: Aberdeen views GSR addition as both appropriate and necessary for ISPs and large enterprise; will complement the 7500 router addressing different needs and markets
notes: Aberdeen's positive prediction on the GSR's market reception.

metric_value: Cisco maintained dominant enterprise networking market position through 2000s and beyond; peaked at $555B market cap (March 2000); retained IOS as de facto standard; still dominant in enterprise networking as of 2026
notes: Aberdeen's central prediction proved entirely correct.

metric_value: Tag Switching evolved into MPLS (RFC 3031, January 2001); MPLS became fundamental to carrier backbone networks and VPNs; still essential to carrier infrastructure as of 2026; Tag Switching itself obsolete but its successor dominant
notes: Aberdeen's hedged prediction was correct in spirit; Tag Switching succeeded via MPLS standardization.

metric_value: Cisco sold 1,000 GSR 12000 units within first year (by November 1998); became major commercial success; backbone routers for major ISPs and carriers; still referenced in carrier networks
notes: GSR success exceeded Aberdeen's already positive prediction.

metric_value: NetFlow v5 became widely deployed standard for network flow monitoring; NetFlow v9 (Flexible NetFlow) became basis for IETF IPFIX standard (RFC 7011, 2013); still fundamental to network monitoring and security analytics as of 2026
notes: NetFlow's monitoring role vastly exceeded its original switching application; became industry standard.

metric_name: Cisco IOS de facto standard persistence
metric_value: Cisco IOS remained the de facto internetworking standard through the 2000s; evolved into IOS XE and IOS XR for modern routing platforms; still the basis of Cisco's networking OS as of 2026
notes: Aberdeen's IOS investment-protection thesis was fully validated over 25+ years.

metric_name: The network is the business thesis
metric_value: Once a mission-critical distributed client-server application is deployed, high-speed network performance is required to sustain it; CEOs recognize network investments create strategic competitive and shareholder value
notes: Aberdeen's framing of the network as a strategic business asset — validated by decades of internet economy development.

metric_value: Cisco acquired StrataCom for $4.67B (June 1996); first true integration was BPX+Stratm Technology+Tag Switching; StrataCom technology became foundation for Cisco's ISP/carrier product line
notes: The StrataCom acquisition paid off as Aberdeen predicted through ISP market penetration.

metric_value: Increased throughput, improved traffic engineering, bandwidth management, QoS support, differentiated Level 3 services, platform and transport flexibility, cost advantage
notes: Aberdeen's articulation of ISP market requirements that would drive Cisco's GSR and MPLS development.

metric_value: 80% of LAN traffic formerly local; now 80% crosses backbone with only 20% remaining in subnet; imbalance demanding correction
notes: Aberdeen documents the network traffic reversal that drove the routing/switching performance crisis.
